mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses 4.5%, or signal loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and consumer prices indicates strong corporate pricing power and robust demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates into positive short-term S&P 500 returns during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during recovery regimes, or 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation (Rank IC < 0.01) with INDPRO growth.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market and rising household income, which consistently supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict return direction with a Rank IC above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp quarterly decline in housing starts signals a collapse in construction demand and broader credit tightening, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in >55% of instances following a >10% housing starts drop, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated federal funds rates and persistent consumer price inflation creates stagflationary pressure that compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields positive returns in >50% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarterly GDP growth confirms macroeconomic expansion and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during stable growth regimes.
failure_condition: GDP growth fails to predict positive returns during mid-cycle expansions, or the signal produces negative returns in >60% of triggered cases over a 3-year rolling window.
